metadata.description: Conservative radar-level aircraft maneuver parameters for Jeju APP training simulation. These values are not certified aircraft performance data.
basis: Use 25 degrees as normal bank reference and 30 degrees as upper training cap.
Compute turn rate from live bank angle and ground speed, capped by max_turn_rate_deg_sec.
Use roll-in and roll-out lag so heading changes do not enter full-rate turns instantly.
Treat controller speed as IAS/KIAS, then convert to no-wind radar ground speed by altitude.
Apply phase modifiers for climb acceleration, high-altitude acceleration, and approach deceleration.
When descent and deceleration targets conflict, shallow the descent before using an altitude hold.
When climb and acceleration targets conflict, reduce climb rate before pretending the aircraft can do both at full performance.
Treat expedite descent as a managed energy mode: higher descent priority and limited speed bias, never overriding speed restrictions.
Limit vertical-rate changes so climb/descent commands do not step instantly between rates.
Taper vertical speed near assigned altitude for smoother altitude capture.

sources.name: EUROCONTROL Base of Aircraft Data (BADA)
sources.use: Reference for real aircraft performance modelling scope; licensed BADA coefficients are not embedded.

sources.name: OpenAP Handbook
sources.use: Open aircraft performance model reference for future refinement.

notes: Default for RKPC arrival/departure training traffic.
Turn rate is derived from live bank angle and current ground speed, then capped by max_turn_rate_deg_sec.
Speed response is intentionally damped for radar-display training feel.
During managed deceleration, descent rate may shallow before an altitude hold is needed.
During climb acceleration, climb rate is reduced to reserve energy for speed.
Expedite descent increases descent priority and may bias speed upward only inside active speed limits.
Controller SPD is modelled as indicated speed; radar movement uses a simple no-wind TAS/GS altitude factor.
Reserved for future turboprop scenario traffic.
